paper_title: Evaluating the performance of Random Forest, Decision Tree, Support Vector Regression and Gradient Boosting for streamflow prediction

entities.description: The scientific study of the movement, distribution, and management of water on Earth.
The process of estimating the volume of water flowing through a river channel over time.
Mathematical representation of the water cycle to simulate river flow and water resources.
An ensemble learning method for regression that operates by constructing a multitude of decision trees.
A non-parametric supervised learning method used for classification and regression.
A type of Support Vector Machine used for regression challenges.
A machine learning technique for regression and classification which produces a prediction model in the form of an ensemble of weak prediction models.
A small-watershed to river basin-scale model used to simulate the quality and quantity of surface and ground water.
A multi-parameter instrument used for acquiring hourly gage height, temperature, and atmospheric pressure.
A normalization technique to scale numerical features to a standardized range between 0 and 1.
